items.q: Why is the cohesion-tension theory critical for water transport in plants?
items.correct: Hydrogen bonds between water molecules keep the water column unbroken under tension, so transpiration can pull water up from the roots.
items.misconception: Cohesion-tension describes how water diffuses freely between cells.
items.random_wrong: Cohesion-tension explains how plants store water in the xylem like a reservoir.
items.why: Students miss the mechanical aspect — that water is under tension (negative pressure) and only hydrogen bonding keeps the column intact.

items.q: How do insulin and glucagon work together to regulate blood glucose?
items.correct: They are antagonistic — insulin lowers blood glucose (cells take up glucose, store as glycogen); glucagon raises it (glycogen broken back down to glucose).
items.misconception: Insulin and glucagon both lower blood glucose using different mechanisms.
items.random_wrong: Insulin works on muscles and glucagon works on the brain.
items.why: Students don't grasp 'antagonistic hormones' — they often think both hormones do similar jobs because both are about glucose.

items.q: What is the role of DNA ligase when inserting a gene into a plasmid?
items.correct: It forms phosphodiester bonds that join the gene to the cut plasmid, sealing the recombinant DNA.
items.misconception: DNA ligase cuts the plasmid open so the gene can be inserted.
items.random_wrong: DNA ligase reads the gene and translates it into protein.
items.why: Students confuse the cutting enzymes (restriction endonucleases) with the joining enzyme (ligase); both are needed but do opposite jobs.

items.q: Which scientist proposed 'omnis cellula e cellula' (all cells from pre-existing cells)?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Virchow. “Schleiden” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane was proposed in 1972 by:
items.why: It's Singer and Nicolson. “Watson and Crick” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Virchow add 'omnis cellula e cellula' to cell theory?
items.why: The real figure is 1858 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
